appeal_letter:
Dear Cigna Appeals Review,

I am writing to appeal the denial of air ambulance transport that occurred on 05/12/2026 for [Member Name]. The denial states that prior authorization was not obtained and that medical necessity for air over ground transport was not established. This appeal asks you to review the emergency circumstances that made prior authorization impossible and ground transport medically contraindicated.

The patient presented to a rural hospital with a massive acute myocardial infarction. The treating facility lacked cardiothoracic surgery capabilities. Immediate transfer to a facility with cardiac catheterization and surgical capability was required to minimize permanent myocardial damage — every additional minute of ischemia results in documented, irreversible tissue loss.

Ground transport was not a clinically viable option. Mountain weather conditions on the date of transfer extended ground transport time to approximately 90 minutes. For a patient in active myocardial infarction, this duration of transport without intervention capability would have resulted in substantially greater cardiac muscle death and materially increased mortality risk. The attending emergency physician documented the air transport order as a life-saving measure, not a convenience.

Cigna's own guidelines recognize that air transport is covered when the time required for ground transport would endanger the patient's life. The documented ground transport time, combined with the acuity of an active cardiac event, satisfies that standard. Prior authorization was not obtained because doing so would have delayed a life-threatening emergency response — which Cigna's policy accounts for.

We request full reversal and coverage of the air ambulance claim. Please confirm receipt and the deadline for any further submission.

This letter is a draft for your review.

feature_scores.evidence: Quotes Cigna's own coverage language on ground transport endangering life.
Two-part framing: emergency exception to prior auth, and medical necessity of air over ground.
90-minute ground transport time is named but not documented with a specific source reference.

gaps: moderate: case_specific_clinical_rebuttal (anchor 3) — weather or road documentation would strengthen the 90-minute claim
outcome.critique: Strong dual framing of the emergency exception; grounding would improve with a source for the 90-minute transport estimate.
rationale: Cigna's own policy language is invoked correctly.
The prior auth exception for genuine emergencies is well-established and directly applicable.

risk_flags: Air ambulance denials are common; the 90-minute ground transport estimate should be supported by documentation to be persuasive.

mirror.what_was_denied: Coverage for air ambulance transport on 05/12/2026.
mirror.why_they_said_no: They say prior authorization was not obtained and that air transport was not medically necessary given the 45-mile distance.
mirror.deadline_note: Check your denial letter for the appeal filing deadline and submit before that date.
mirror.strongest_angle: Mountain weather made ground transport 90 minutes during an active heart attack — that directly meets Cigna's own standard for when air transport is covered.

citations_used.title: Cigna guidelines (referenced in denial)
citations_used.quote: air ambulance transport is only covered when ground transport is medically contraindicated or the time required for ground transport would endanger the patient's life

citations_used.title: Denial letter
citations_used.quote: Our records show the distance between the two hospitals was only 45 miles

missing_evidence_checklist: ER physician's transport order documenting the life-threatening rationale
Weather or road condition records for the date of transport supporting the 90-minute estimate
Documentation that the originating hospital lacked cardiac catheterization capability
